mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations and producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0 percent within a single month, it signals an abrupt deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional IC drops below -0.05 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions turn sharply negative.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines >1.0% month-over-month and HOUST declines >2.0% month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid inflation acceleration, aggressive federal funds rate hikes fail to stabilize equity valuations because real rates remain negative, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 20 trading days as discount rate uncertainty spikes.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the slope coefficient on FEDFUNDS in a rolling regression does not turn significantly negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real personal consumption expenditures reflects robust aggregate demand that directly supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as earnings momentum accelerates.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the rank correlation between RSAFS changes and SPX returns falls below -0.02 over a 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index exceeding 1.5 percent signals a supply-side rebound that lowers input cost pressures and boosts operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered months, or the cross-sectional information coefficient remains below 0.04 across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth and a contraction in housing starts indicates a labor-driven demand shift away from rate-sensitive sectors, compressing growth stock valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises >150k MoM and HOUST falls >5% MoM
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates below 1.0 percent annualized while the federal funds rate remains above 3.0 percent, the policy stance becomes excessively restrictive for the prevailing growth trajectory, triggering a negative earnings revision cycle and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the slope coefficient on GDPC1 in a rolling regression does not turn significantly negative when FEDFUNDS exceeds the threshold.
